result:
All good. Here's a summary of what was changed:

## Changes Made

### `/root/life-simulator/life/modes/traffic.py` — Complete rewrite
The existing basic single-lane NaSch model was replaced with a full-featured multi-lane traffic simulation:

- **Multi-lane lane-changing** (STCA symmetric rule): vehicles check adjacent lanes for better gaps, considering safety distance to cars behind
- **10 presets** covering diverse scenarios:
  - Open Highway, Moderate Flow, Rush Hour, Gridlock (periodic ring road at various densities)
  - **Bottleneck** — speed limit drops to 2 in the center third, causing flow breakdown
  - **On-Ramp Merge** — slip road injects cars into lane 0, disrupting mainline flow
  - **Incident** — stalled vehicle (permanent obstacle) blocks a lane, causing rubbernecking cascade
  - Cautious/Aggressive drivers (varying dawdling probability)
  - Wide Highway (8 lanes)
- **Real-time fundamental diagram overlay** — scatter plot of flow (J) vs. density (ρ) drawn in ASCII on the right side, showing the characteristic inverted-V phase transition
- **Space-time diagram view** (`v` to toggle) — shows propagation of jam waves as dark bands traveling upstream
- **New controls**: `v`=view toggle, `f`=diagram toggle, `l`=lane-change toggle
- Removed dead Fluid LBM constants that were orphaned in this file

### `/root/life-simulator/life/app.py` — State initialization
Added new state variables for lane-changing, fundamental diagram history, space-time buffer, on-ramp/bottleneck/incident parameters.

### `/root/life-simulator/life/registry.py` — Updated description
Updated the mode description to reflect the enhanced capabilities.
